description: Phase 2 - Replace all toy cryptography in @quant/auth with production-grade implementations. Replace djb2 hash signing with jose HMAC-SHA256, replace Math.random() with crypto.randomBytes/randomInt, implement argon2id password hashing, implement real PKCE S256 with crypto.subtle, add TOTP via otplib, implement refresh token rotation with reuse detection backed by Prisma, add JWKS endpoint support, and add WebAuthn passkey support. Add comprehensive tests.
steps: Step 1: Install required dependencies. Add to packages/auth/package.json: jose (JWT/JWS/JWKS), argon2 (password hashing), otplib (TOTP), @simplewebauthn/server (WebAuthn). Add @types/node as devDependency. Run pnpm install from workspace root.

Step 2: Create packages/auth/src/crypto/secure-random.ts - A utility module that exports functions replacing ALL Math.random() usage: generateSecureToken(length: number): string (hex using crypto.randomBytes), generateSecureCode(length: number): string (numeric OTP using crypto.randomInt), generateId(prefix: string): string (e.g. 'tok_' + crypto.randomBytes(16).toString('hex')). All crypto uses Node.js built-in 'node:crypto'.

Step 3: Create packages/auth/src/crypto/password.ts - Implements PasswordService class with: hash(password: string): Promise<string> using argon2id with OWASP params (memoryCost: 19456, timeCost: 2, parallelism: 1), verify(hash: string, password: string): Promise<boolean>, needsRehash(hash: string): boolean. Export a singleton instance.

Step 4: Create packages/auth/src/crypto/pkce.ts - Implements PKCE utilities: generateCodeVerifier(): string (43-128 char random string), generateCodeChallenge(verifier: string): Promise<string> (SHA-256 + base64url), validateCodeChallenge(verifier: string, challenge: string, method: 'plain' | 'S256'): Promise<boolean>. Uses Node.js crypto.subtle for SHA-256.

Step 5: Create packages/auth/src/crypto/totp.ts - Implements TOTPService class using otplib: generateSecret(): string, generateQRCodeUri(secret: string, email: string, issuer?: string): string, verify(token: string, secret: string): boolean (with 1-step window), generateBackupCodes(count?: number): string[] (8 codes, each 8 chars hex from crypto.randomBytes).
Step 6: Create packages/auth/src/crypto/index.ts - barrel export for all crypto modules.

Step 7: Rewrite packages/auth/src/services/token-service.ts completely. Replace the encodeToken/decodeToken/sign methods with jose library: use new jose.SignJWT(...).setProtectedHeader({alg:'HS256'}).setIssuedAt().setExpirationTime().setIssuer().setAudience().setJti().sign(secret) for access tokens. Use jose.jwtVerify(token, secret, { issuer, audience }) for validation. Replace generateTokenId() with the new secure-random generateId('tok'). Keep the refresh token family tracking logic but use secure IDs. Add JWKS support: generateKeyPair() for RS256, exportJWKS() method that returns a JWKS document, signWithPrivateKey() for federation tokens.

Step 8: Rewrite packages/auth/src/services/session-service.ts. Replace the generateSessionId() method to use crypto.randomBytes. Keep the in-memory Maps for now (they will be replaced by Prisma in a repository pattern, but the interface stays the same). The key fix is replacing Math.random() with crypto.

Step 9: Rewrite packages/auth/src/providers/quantmail-provider.ts. Replace generateSecureCode() and generateClientSecret() to use crypto.randomBytes. Replace sha256Base64Url() with the real PKCE validateCodeChallenge from the new crypto/pkce module. Import and use the new secure-random utilities throughout.

Step 10: Rewrite packages/auth/src/providers/phone-provider.ts. Replace generateVerificationCode() to use crypto.randomInt for each digit. Replace Math.random() in deliverSMS messageId generation with crypto.randomBytes.

Step 11: Create packages/auth/src/services/webauthn-service.ts - WebAuthn passkey support using @simplewebauthn/server: generateRegistrationOptions(userId, username, existingCredentials), verifyRegistration(response, expectedChallenge, expectedOrigin, expectedRPID), generateAuthenticationOptions(existingCredentials), verifyAuthentication(response, expectedChallenge, expectedOrigin, expectedRPID, credential). Store challenges in a Map (can be replaced with Redis later).
Step 12: Update packages/auth/src/index.ts to export all new modules: crypto utilities (PasswordService, TOTPService, PKCE utils, secure-random), WebAuthnService.

Step 13: Update packages/auth/src/types.ts to add WebAuthn types: WebAuthnCredential { credentialId: string, publicKey: string, counter: number, transports: string[], createdAt: Date }, WebAuthnRegistrationOptions, WebAuthnAuthenticationOptions.

Step 14: Create packages/auth/src/__tests__/token-service.test.ts with tests: generates valid JWT that can be verified with jose.jwtVerify, validates token claims (sub, email, scopes, iss, aud, jti, exp), rejects expired tokens, rejects tokens with wrong issuer/audience, refresh token rotation returns new pair and invalidates old, reuse detection revokes entire family, JWKS export returns valid JWK set.

Step 15: Create packages/auth/src/__tests__/password.test.ts with tests: hashes password with argon2id, verifies correct password, rejects wrong password, hash output contains $argon2id$ prefix, different passwords produce different hashes.

Step 16: Create packages/auth/src/__tests__/pkce.test.ts with tests: generates verifier of correct length, generates challenge that is base64url encoded, validates correct verifier against S256 challenge, rejects wrong verifier, plain method works as simple equality.

Step 17: Create packages/auth/src/__tests__/totp.test.ts with tests: generates base32 secret, generates valid otpauth:// URI, verifies a valid token generated from the secret, rejects invalid token, generates backup codes of correct format (8 hex chars each, 8 codes).

Step 18: Create packages/auth/src/__tests__/secure-random.test.ts with tests: generateSecureToken returns hex string of correct length, generateSecureCode returns numeric string, generateId returns string with correct prefix, all values are unique across multiple calls.
Step 19: Add packages/auth/vitest.config.ts (similar to database package) and update packages/auth/package.json scripts to include 'test': 'vitest run'.
Step 20: Run pnpm install, then run the tests in packages/auth to verify everything passes. Fix any issues.
acceptance_criteria: All Math.random() calls removed from packages/auth/src/ - grep confirms zero occurrences
All djb2/toy hash functions removed - no manual bit-shifting hash implementations remain
Token signing uses jose library with HS256 algorithm
Token verification uses jose.jwtVerify with proper claim validation
Password hashing uses argon2id with OWASP-recommended parameters
PKCE S256 uses real SHA-256 via crypto.subtle
TOTP uses otplib with proper secret generation
All random values generated via node:crypto (randomBytes or randomInt)
WebAuthn service exists with registration and authentication flows
JWKS endpoint method returns a valid JSON Web Key Set
All new tests pass (pnpm test from auth package)
TypeScript compiles cleanly (pnpm typecheck from auth package)
verification: Run: cd packages/auth && npx vitest run (all auth tests pass)
Run: npx tsc --noEmit -p packages/auth/tsconfig.json (no type errors)
Run: grep -r 'Math.random' packages/auth/src/ (returns empty - no occurrences)
Run: grep -rn 'hash << 5\|hash.*<<.*5' packages/auth/src/ (returns empty - no djb2)
findings: 1. jose v6 uses CryptoKey type instead of KeyLike. 2. argon2 is CJS-only, default import works with esModuleInterop. 3. argon2.needsRehash does not accept type param, only timeCost/memoryCost/parallelism/version. 4. TypeScript composite project references require tsc -b for typecheck (not --noEmit with -p) due to TS6305 errors about unbuilt output files from referenced projects. 5. Refresh token reuse detection: when token not found in activeRefreshTokens but family exists, treat as reuse and revoke family. 6. NODE_OPTIONS must be unset to avoid proxy-bootstrap.js errors in git hooks and pnpm.
